mnemonics.title: Kruskal's Algorithm Steps
mnemonics.content: Kruskal's: SORT, THEN CUT - Sort edges by weight, use Union-Find to Cut cycles

mnemonics.title: Prim's Algorithm
mnemonics.content: PRIM: Pick vertex, Repeat - Extract minimum edge connecting tree to new vertex

mnemonics.title: Kruskal vs Prim
mnemonics.content: Kruskal: Edges sorted, forest grows. Prim: One tree, grows from root.

acronyms.usage: A spanning tree with minimum total edge weight in a weighted graph
Data structure used in Kruskal's algorithm to detect cycles
In graph theory, V represents the count of vertices in a graph
In graph theory, E represents the count of edges in a graph

visualTips.visualization: Imagine cutting a graph into two pieces with scissors. The lightest edge crossing the cut (connecting the two pieces) MUST be in the MST. Draw a line through the graph to see this.
If you find a cycle (a loop), the heaviest wire in that loop can be cut to get a lighter or equal tree. Think of removing the most expensive wire from a circuit loop.
Think of Kruskal's building a forest that gradually merges into one tree. Each edge is a potential bridge between two islands (components).
Think of Prim's like a growing tree or vine. Starting from a seed (vertex), it extends one branch at a time, always picking the cheapest branch that reaches new ground.

analogies.analogy: Like building a road network by first paving the cheapest roads between any two towns, merging small regions into larger ones, until all towns are connected.
Like laying electricity cables from a power station, always extending to the nearest unconnected house using the shortest available cable.
Like a gate separating two fields - the cheapest fence to cross that gate MUST be used in any optimal fence design.
Like designing an airline route map that connects all cities with minimum total fuel cost, without any unnecessary circular routes.
Like finding a redundant wire in a circuit - if you have a loop, removing the most expensive wire always makes the circuit cheaper or equally cheap.
